This extensive property offers more than a museum with external Egyptian decoration with  a planetarium, peaceful peace garden, a fountain, research library, Pythagoras statue, the ancient game of passing, and Francis Bacon Auditorium.  The signs are legible to mark the path through the grounds.  This is a complete Egyptian museum with various media
This multi-media  museum offers a complete intro film with travels through various ages of Egypt, inc. vocabulary and mummies, but dryers from men’s and women's restrooms are so that they drown the film.
Display case signs are legible and at accessible height to cover the major sites and elements of historical importance and cultural elements. They include coverage of daily life, royalty, funerals, and death rituals, jewelry, adornment, animals and leisure pursuits through statues, carvings, pottery, paintings, and multi media.
one of the statues of Cleopatra VII is one of only 7 known in the world 
The only challenge is a beautiful vertical display of 12 plaques  showing historical eras go up to the sky and hard to read on the top plaques.
Today is a school field trip with kids doing scavenger hunts and getting their museum passports stamped at various stations.
The museum is set on several levels with wheel chairlifts  on all levels except the underground tomb with 7 to 11 steps.
Accessible mens and women’s restrooms with dedicated stall 2 grab bars 3ft x 5 ft space, Accessible free street parallel parking
Children under 6 free, adult child, and senior fees with seaon pass available.

Great experience at this museum. Well worth the entry price and drive to get here. I and my teenage daughter spent several hours here and would definitely come back! Make sure to bring your headphones or earbuds so you can utilize the museum app.
Impressive collection, especially the mummies and the recreated tomb, which was a highlight. The grounds and gardens also make it a very peaceful place to spend a few hours.
